rawText:
How do you explain when things don't go as we assume? Or better, how do you explain when others are able to achieve things that seem to defy all of the assumptions? For example: Why is Apple so innovative? Year after year, after year, they're more innovative than all their competition. And yet, they're just a computer company. They're just like everyone else. They have the same access to the same talent, the same agencies, the same consultants, the same media. Then why is it that they seem to have something different?

Why is it that Martin Luther King led the Civil Rights Movement? He wasn't the only man who suffered in pre-civil rights America, and he certainly wasn't the only great orator of the day. Why him? And why is it that the Wright brothers were able to figure out controlled, powered man flight when there were certainly other teams who were better qualified, better funded—and they didn't achieve powered man flight, and the Wright brothers beat them to it. There's something else at play here.

About three and a half years ago, I made a discovery. And this discovery profoundly changed my view on how I thought the world worked, and it even profoundly changed the way in which I operate in it. As it turns out, there's a pattern. As it turns out, all the great inspiring leaders and organizations in the world, whether it's Apple or Martin Luther King or the Wright brothers, they all think, act and communicate the exact same way. And it's the complete opposite to everyone else. All I did was codify it, and it's probably the world's simplest idea. I call it the golden circle.

Why? How? What? This little idea explains why some organizations and some leaders are able to inspire where others aren't. Let me define the terms really quickly. Every single person, every single organization on the planet knows what they do, 100 percent. Some know how they do it, whether you call it your differentiated value proposition or your proprietary process or your USP. But very, very few people or organizations know why they do what they do. And by "why" I don't mean "to make a profit." That's a result. It's always a result. By "why," I mean: What's your purpose? What's your cause? What's your belief? Why does your organization exist? Why do you get out of bed in the morning? And why should anyone care?

As a result, the way we think, we act, the way we communicate is from the outside in, it's obvious. We go from the clearest thing to the fuzziest thing. But the inspired leaders and the inspired organizations—regardless of their size, regardless of their industry—all think, act and communicate from the inside out.

sentences.original: How do you explain when things don't go as we assume?
sentences.translation: 일이 우리가 예상한 대로 되지 않을 때 어떻게 설명하시겠습니까?

grammarElements.text: when things don't go as we assume
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (시간/조건 — when)

vocabulary.example: Can you explain why this happened?
We assumed the project would be easy.

expressions.usage: 청중에게 질문을 던져 사고를 유도할 때

expressions.phrase: things don't go as we assume
expressions.usage: 기대와 현실의 괴리를 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: How do you explain when [situation]?
patterns.explanation: 특정 상황에 대한 설명을 요구하는 수사적 질문 패턴. TED 강연에서 청중의 호기심을 유발하는 오프닝으로 사용.
examples: How do you explain when a small company beats a giant?
How do you explain when hard work doesn't lead to success?

drillQuestions.question: How do you explain _____ things don't go as planned?
Things don't go _____ we assume.

sentences.notes: 강연의 첫 문장으로, 수사적 질문(rhetorical question)을 던져 청중의 호기심을 유도한다. "as we assume"에서 as는 "~하듯이, ~대로"라는 비교/양태의 접속사.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 수사적 질문(rhetorical question) — 답을 기대하지 않는 질문으로 청중의 사고를 자극하며 강연을 시작

sentences.original: Or better, how do you explain when others are able to achieve things that seem to defy all of the assumptions?
sentences.translation: 더 나아가, 다른 사람들이 모든 가정을 뒤엎는 것처럼 보이는 것들을 성취할 수 있을 때 어떻게 설명하시겠습니까?

grammarElements.text: when others are able to achieve things
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (when)

grammarElements.text: that seem to defy all of the assumptions
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (that — things를 수식)

vocabulary.example: She achieved her goal of running a marathon.
The results defied all expectations.
We shouldn't make assumptions without evidence.

expressions.meaning: 더 나아가, 아니 더 좋은 질문은
expressions.usage: 이전 질문을 더 강한 질문으로 업그레이드할 때
능력과 결과를 동시에 표현할 때 (can보다 격식)

patterns.pattern: Or better, [stronger version of previous question]
patterns.explanation: 이전 질문을 더 강력한 버전으로 바꿔 제시하는 패턴. 논점을 점진적으로 날카롭게 만든다.
examples: Or better, why do some people succeed where everyone else fails?
Or better, how did they do it with half the resources?

drillQuestions.question: Others are able to achieve things that seem to _____ all assumptions.
Things that seem _____ defy all of the assumptions.

sentences.notes: "seem to defy"에서 seem은 "~인 것처럼 보이다"로, 화자의 주관적 판단을 부드럽게 만드는 hedging(완화) 표현. defy는 "법이나 규칙에 저항하다"에서 "기대를 뒤엎다"로 확장된 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: when others are able to achieve things
koreanAlignment.ko: 다른 사람들이 것들을 성취할 수 있을 때

koreanAlignment.en: that seem to defy all of the assumptions

sentences.original: For example: Why is Apple so innovative?
sentences.translation: 예를 들어: 왜 Apple은 그렇게 혁신적일까요?

vocabulary.example: The company is known for its innovative products.

expressions.usage: 구체적인 사례를 제시하기 전에 사용하는 담화 표지

patterns.pattern: Why is [subject] so [adjective]?
patterns.explanation: 특정 대상의 뛰어난 특성에 대해 질문하는 패턴. so가 형용사를 강조.
examples: Why is Tesla so popular?
Why is this approach so effective?

drillQuestions.question: Why is Apple _____ innovative?

sentences.notes: 짧고 직접적인 질문으로 청중의 공감을 이끌어낸다. Apple은 2009년 당시에도 혁신의 대명사였으며, 청중 모두가 공감할 수 있는 예시.

sentences.original: Year after year, after year, they're more innovative than all their competition.
sentences.translation: 해마다, 또 해마다, 그들은 모든 경쟁사보다 더 혁신적입니다.

vocabulary.example: They need to stay ahead of the competition.

expressions.usage: 오랜 기간 지속되는 반복을 강조할 때
비교급으로 우위를 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: [Time] after [time], after [time], [subject] [comparative] than ...
patterns.explanation: 시간 표현을 반복하여 지속성을 강조하는 패턴. 세 번 반복으로 리듬감을 만든다.
examples: Day after day, after day, they kept trying.
Time after time, after time, the same mistake was repeated.

drillQuestions.question: Year _____ year, they are more innovative.
They're more innovative _____ all their competition.

sentences.notes: "year after year"를 한 번 더 반복하여 "after year"를 추가한 것은 사이넥의 수사적 기법. 표준 표현은 "year after year"이지만, 세 번째 반복으로 청중에게 지속성의 인상을 더 강하게 남긴다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 반복법(epizeuxis) — "year after year, after year"의 삼중 반복으로 지속적 혁신을 강조

sentences.original: And yet, they're just a computer company. They're just like everyone else.
sentences.translation: 그런데도, 그들은 그저 컴퓨터 회사일 뿐입니다. 다른 모든 회사와 다를 바 없습니다.

vocabulary.example: He was tired, and yet he kept working.

expressions.usage: 앞선 내용과 모순되는 사실을 소개할 때
차별성이 없음을 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: And yet, [subject] [be] just [noun]. [Subject] [be] just like everyone else.
patterns.explanation: 뛰어난 성과와 평범한 조건의 모순을 강조하는 패턴. 청중의 호기심을 극대화.
examples: And yet, they're just a small startup. They're just like everyone else.
And yet, he was just a student. He was just like everyone else.

drillQuestions.question: And _____, they are just a computer company.
They're just _____ everyone else.

sentences.notes: "just"가 두 번 사용되어 Apple의 "평범함"을 의도적으로 강조한다. 앞서 말한 혁신성과의 대비(juxtaposition)를 통해 "그렇다면 비결은 무엇인가?"라는 질문을 자연스럽게 유도.

koreanAlignment.ko: 다른 모든 회사와 다를 바 없습니다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 병치/대조(juxtaposition) — 혁신적이라는 평가와 "그저 컴퓨터 회사"라는 현실을 나란히 놓아 모순을 부각

sentences.original: They have the same access to the same talent, the same agencies, the same consultants, the same media.
sentences.translation: 그들은 같은 인재, 같은 대행사, 같은 컨설턴트, 같은 미디어에 똑같이 접근할 수 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: to the same talent, the same agencies, the same consultants, the same media
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (to — 나열/병렬)

vocabulary.example: Everyone should have access to education.
The company hired a management consultant.

expressions.usage: 조건의 동등함을 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: [Subject] have the same [A], the same [B], the same [C], the same [D].
patterns.explanation: "the same"을 반복하여 조건의 동등함을 리듬감 있게 나열하는 패턴.
examples: They had the same training, the same tools, the same budget, the same timeline.

drillQuestions.question: They have the same _____ to the same talent.
They have the same talent, the same agencies, the _____ consultants.

sentences.notes: "the same"이 5번 반복되는 열거법(enumeration). 동일한 조건임에도 Apple만 다른 결과를 내는 점을 부각하여 청중의 궁금증을 키운다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 열거법(enumeration) + 반복법(anaphora) — "the same"을 반복하여 동일 조건을 강조

sentences.original: Then why is it that they seem to have something different?
sentences.translation: 그렇다면 왜 그들은 뭔가 다른 것을 가지고 있는 것처럼 보일까요?

vocabulary.example: Things are not always what they seem.

expressions.usage: 단순한 "why"보다 강한 의문을 표현하는 분열문(cleft sentence)
구체적으로 규정할 수 없는 차별성을 암시할 때

patterns.pattern: Why is it that [subject] [verb] ...?
patterns.explanation: "why is it that" 분열문은 단순 "why"보다 강한 의문을 표현. 강연에서 핵심 질문을 제기할 때 효과적.
examples: Why is it that some people always succeed?
Why is it that we keep making the same mistakes?

drillQuestions.question: Why is _____ that they seem to have something different?
They seem _____ have something different.

sentences.notes: "why is it that"은 분열문(cleft sentence)으로, 단순 "why do they have"보다 강한 의문과 강조를 만든다. 동일 조건을 나열한 뒤 던지는 결론적 질문으로 첫 문단을 마무리.
pronunciationNotes: different: /ˈdɪfrənt/ — "디프런트", 구어에서 가운데 음절이 탈락하여 2음절로 발음되기도 함

sentences.original: Why is it that Martin Luther King led the Civil Rights Movement?
sentences.translation: 왜 마틴 루서 킹이 시민권 운동을 이끌었을까요?

vocabulary.example: She led the team to victory.
The Civil Rights Movement changed American history.

expressions.usage: 1950-60년대 미국의 인종 평등 운동을 지칭

patterns.pattern: Why is it that [person] [past tense verb] [historical event]?
patterns.explanation: 역사적 인물과 사건에 대한 근본적 질문을 던지는 패턴.
examples: Why is it that Gandhi led the independence movement?
Why is it that Steve Jobs revolutionized technology?

drillQuestions.question: Martin Luther King _____ the Civil Rights Movement.

sentences.notes: lead-led-led 불규칙 동사 변화에 주의. lead(/liːd/)의 과거형 led(/led/)는 금속 lead(/led/, 납)와 같은 발음. 사이넥은 Apple에서 King으로, 기업에서 인물로 예시를 확장한다.

sentences.original: He wasn't the only man who suffered in pre-civil rights America, and he certainly wasn't the only great orator of the day.
sentences.translation: 그는 시민권 이전 시대 미국에서 고통받은 유일한 사람이 아니었고, 당대의 유일한 위대한 연설가도 분명 아니었습니다.

grammarElements.text: who suffered in pre-civil rights America
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (who — man을 수식)

grammarElements.text: wasn't the only great orator of the day
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: Many people suffered during the war.
He was known as one of the greatest orators of his time.
Pre-civil rights America was deeply segregated.

expressions.usage: 특정 인물의 독점적 지위를 부정할 때
특정 시대의 대표적 인물/사물을 지칭할 때

patterns.pattern: [Subject] wasn't the only [person] who [verb], and [subject] certainly wasn't the only [noun].
patterns.explanation: 특정 인물이 유일하지 않았음을 두 번 강조하여, "그렇다면 왜 그 사람인가?"라는 질문을 유도하는 패턴.
examples: She wasn't the only scientist who studied the problem, and she certainly wasn't the only one with funding.

drillQuestions.question: He wasn't the only man _____ suffered in pre-civil rights America.
He certainly wasn't the only great _____ of the day.

sentences.notes: orator는 "연설가"라는 뜻의 격식체 어휘. oration(연설), oratory(웅변술)와 같은 어족. "of the day"는 "당대의"라는 관용적 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: who suffered in pre-civil rights America
wasn't the only great orator of the day

sentences.original: And why is it that the Wright brothers were able to figure out controlled, powered man flight when there were certainly other teams who were better qualified, better funded—and they didn't achieve powered man flight, and the Wright brothers beat them to it.
sentences.translation: 그리고 왜 라이트 형제가 유인 동력 비행을 알아냈을까요? 분명히 더 자격이 있고, 더 많은 자금을 가진 다른 팀들이 있었는데—그들은 동력 비행을 이루지 못했고, 라이트 형제가 먼저 해냈습니다.

grammarElements.text: when there were certainly other teams
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (양보적 when)

grammarElements.text: who were better qualified, better funded
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절

grammarElements.text: they didn't achieve powered man flight
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (대조)

grammarElements.text: the Wright brothers beat them to it
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (결과)

vocabulary.example: We need to figure out a solution.
She is well qualified for the job.
The project was well funded by the government.
I wanted to apply, but she beat me to it.

expressions.usage: 어려운 문제를 해결하거나 이해할 때
경쟁에서 앞서다는 것을 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: [Subject A] were able to [verb] when [Subject B] who were better [adjective] didn't, and [A] beat them to it.
patterns.explanation: 열세인 주체가 우세한 경쟁자를 이겼다는 역설적 상황을 설명하는 패턴.
examples: The small team was able to innovate when larger teams who were better resourced couldn't, and they beat them to it.

drillQuestions.question: The Wright brothers were able to figure _____ powered man flight.
The Wright brothers _____ them to it.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 강연에서 가장 긴 문장 중 하나. "beat someone to it"은 "다른 사람보다 먼저 해내다"라는 관용 표현. 대시(—)를 사용하여 부가 정보를 삽입하는 구어적 문체.

koreanAlignment.en: why is it that the Wright brothers were able to figure out
when there were certainly other teams
who were better qualified, better funded
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 자격이 있고, 더 많은 자금을 가진

koreanAlignment.en: they didn't achieve powered man flight
the Wright brothers beat them to it

sentences.original: There's something else at play here.
sentences.translation: 여기에는 뭔가 다른 것이 작용하고 있습니다.

vocabulary.example: There are many factors at play in this situation.

expressions.usage: 표면적 설명으로는 부족하고 더 깊은 원인이 있음을 암시할 때

patterns.pattern: There's something [else/more] at play [here].
patterns.explanation: 보이지 않는 요인이 있음을 암시하는 전환 문장. 다음 내용(해답)으로 자연스럽게 연결.
examples: There's something more at play in this crisis.
There's something else at play behind these numbers.

drillQuestions.question: There's something else at _____ here.

sentences.notes: "at play"는 "작동 중인, 영향을 미치는"이라는 뜻의 관용구. 이 짧은 문장은 문제 제기(paragraphs 0-1)에서 답(paragraph 2)으로 전환하는 다리 역할.
pronunciationNotes: at play: 두 단어를 자연스럽게 연결하여 /ətˈpleɪ/로 발음
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 전환(transition) — 질문 나열에서 해답 제시로의 전환을 예고하는 짧은 문장

sentences.original: About three and a half years ago, I made a discovery.
sentences.translation: 약 3년 반 전에, 저는 하나의 발견을 했습니다.

grammarElements.text: About three and a half years ago
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사구

vocabulary.example: The discovery of penicillin saved millions of lives.

expressions.usage: "discover"를 명사 구문으로 표현하여 발견의 중요성을 부각

expressions.phrase: about three and a half years ago
expressions.usage: 정확하지 않은 과거 시점을 about으로 표현

patterns.pattern: [Time expression] ago, I [past tense verb] [important noun].
patterns.explanation: 개인적 경험을 시간 표현과 함께 도입하는 스토리텔링 패턴.
examples: About five years ago, I made a decision that changed everything.
A few months ago, I had an experience that opened my eyes.

drillQuestions.question: About three and a half years _____, I made a discovery.
I _____ a discovery that changed my view.

sentences.notes: "make a discovery"는 기능동사(light verb) 구문. "discover"보다 "make a discovery"가 발견의 순간을 더 극적으로 전달한다. 개인 내러티브 시작.

koreanAlignment.en: About three and a half years ago

sentences.original: And this discovery profoundly changed my view on how I thought the world worked, and it even profoundly changed the way in which I operate in it.
sentences.translation: 그리고 이 발견은 제가 세상이 작동한다고 생각했던 관점을 근본적으로 바꿨고, 제가 세상에서 행동하는 방식마저도 근본적으로 바꿨습니다.

grammarElements.text: my view on how I thought the world worked
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (간접의문문 내포)

grammarElements.text: the way in which I operate in it
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (관계대명사절)

vocabulary.example: The experience profoundly affected her.
The company operates in 30 countries.
This changed my view on education.

expressions.usage: 깊은 변화를 강조할 때
"the way"의 격식적 표현 (구어에서는 "the way" 또는 "how")

patterns.pattern: This [noun] profoundly changed [A], and it even profoundly changed [B].
patterns.explanation: "profoundly changed"를 두 번 반복하고, 두 번째에 "even"을 추가하여 영향의 깊이를 점진적으로 확대하는 패턴.
examples: This experience profoundly changed my career, and it even profoundly changed my personal relationships.

drillQuestions.question: This discovery _____ changed my view on the world.
It changed the way _____ which I operate in it.

sentences.notes: "how I thought the world worked"는 간접의문문이 이중으로 내포된 구조: view on [how [I thought [the world worked]]]. "the way in which"는 "the way that" 또는 "how"와 같은 의미의 격식체.

koreanAlignment.en: my view on how I thought the world worked
the way in which I operate in it

sentences.original: As it turns out, all the great inspiring leaders and organizations in the world, whether it's Apple or Martin Luther King or the Wright brothers, they all think, act and communicate the exact same way.
sentences.translation: 알고 보니, 세상의 모든 위대한 영감을 주는 리더와 조직들은, Apple이든 마틴 루서 킹이든 라이트 형제든, 모두 정확히 같은 방식으로 생각하고, 행동하고, 소통합니다.

grammarElements.text: all the great inspiring leaders and organizations in the world
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: whether it's Apple or Martin Luther King or the Wright brothers
grammarElements.label: 삽입절 (whether — 양보)

vocabulary.example: She told an inspiring story of perseverance.
Great leaders know how to communicate effectively.
That is the exact same thing I was thinking.

expressions.usage: 예상치 못한 발견이나 결론을 소개할 때
완전한 동일성을 강조할 때. exact가 same을 강화

patterns.pattern: As it turns out, [all X], whether it's [A] or [B] or [C], they all [verb] the exact same way.
patterns.explanation: 발견된 패턴을 제시하고, 다양한 예시를 whether로 나열한 후, 공통점을 강조하는 패턴.
examples: As it turns out, all successful companies, whether it's Google or Amazon or Tesla, they all follow the same principle.

drillQuestions.question: As it _____ out, there is a pattern.
They all think, act and communicate the _____ same way.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 강연의 핵심 주장(thesis statement). 긴 주어 뒤에 삽입절(whether ...)이 오고, 다시 "they all"로 주어를 반복하는 것은 구어의 특징. 앞서 제시한 세 예시(Apple, King, Wright)를 하나로 묶는다.

koreanAlignment.en: all the great inspiring leaders and organizations
koreanAlignment.ko: 모든 위대한 영감을 주는 리더와 조직들은

koreanAlignment.en: whether it's Apple or Martin Luther King or the Wright brothers
koreanAlignment.ko: Apple이든 마틴 루서 킹이든 라이트 형제든

koreanAlignment.en: they all think, act and communicate

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 열거법(enumeration) + 통합(synthesis) — 세 가지 예시를 하나의 패턴으로 통합하여 핵심 주장을 제시

sentences.original: And it's the complete opposite to everyone else.
sentences.translation: 그리고 그것은 다른 모든 사람과 완전히 반대입니다.

vocabulary.example: He did the complete opposite of what was expected.
It was a complete surprise.

expressions.usage: 극단적 대비를 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: And it's the complete opposite to [everyone/everything else].
patterns.explanation: 앞선 주장을 더욱 극적으로 만드는 짧은 보충 문장. 대비를 극대화.
examples: And it's the complete opposite to how most people think.
And it's the complete opposite to what we were taught.

drillQuestions.question: It's the complete _____ to everyone else.

sentences.notes: 짧고 임팩트 있는 문장으로 핵심 대비를 완성. "opposite to"는 영국식 표현이며, 미국식으로는 "opposite of"도 사용된다.

sentences.original: All I did was codify it, and it's probably the world's simplest idea.
sentences.translation: 제가 한 것이라곤 그것을 체계화한 것이고, 아마도 세상에서 가장 단순한 아이디어일 것입니다.

grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계대명사 what 생략 = what I did)

vocabulary.example: The rules were codified into a formal document.

expressions.usage: 자신의 기여를 겸손하게 축소하여 표현할 때
소유격 최상급 표현으로 극단적 단순함을 강조

patterns.pattern: All I did was [verb], and it's probably the [superlative] [noun].
patterns.explanation: 자신의 역할을 겸손하게 표현한 뒤, 아이디어의 단순함을 최상급으로 강조하는 패턴.
examples: All I did was connect the dots, and it's probably the most obvious conclusion.
All we did was listen, and it's probably the easiest thing to do.

drillQuestions.question: All I did was _____ it.
It's probably the world's _____ idea.

sentences.notes: "All I did was codify it"의 구조: All (that) I did = 내가 한 모든 것. was 뒤에 원형부정사(codify)가 오는 것은 의사분열문(pseudo-cleft). codify는 code(법전) + -ify(동사 접미사)로, "체계화하다"라는 학술적 어휘.

koreanAlignment.en: All I did was codify it
koreanAlignment.ko: 제가 한 것이라곤 그것을 체계화한 것뿐입니다

sentences.original: I call it the golden circle.
sentences.translation: 저는 그것을 골든 서클이라고 부릅니다.

vocabulary.example: This is a golden opportunity.

expressions.usage: 새로운 개념에 이름을 붙여 소개할 때
사이넥의 핵심 프레임워크 명칭. golden은 "황금의"에서 "핵심적인, 가장 중요한"의 비유적 의미

patterns.pattern: I call it the [adjective] [noun].
patterns.explanation: 새로운 개념이나 프레임워크에 기억하기 쉬운 이름을 부여하는 패턴. 브랜딩 기법.
examples: I call it the ripple effect.
I call it the 80/20 principle.

drillQuestions.question: I _____ it the golden circle.

sentences.notes: 5형식 문장(SVOC): call(V) + it(O) + the golden circle(OC). 강연에서 핵심 아이디어에 고유한 이름을 붙이는 것은 기억과 확산에 효과적인 전략. "Golden Circle"은 이 강연 이후 경영학 용어로 자리잡았다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 명명(naming) — 새로운 개념에 "골든 서클"이라는 이름을 부여하여 기억성과 전파력을 높임

sentences.original: This little idea explains why some organizations and some leaders are able to inspire where others aren't.
sentences.translation: 이 작은 아이디어가 왜 어떤 조직과 리더는 영감을 줄 수 있는데 다른 이들은 그렇지 못한지를 설명합니다.

grammarElements.text: why some organizations and some leaders are able to inspire
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (간접의문문 — why)

vocabulary.example: Great leaders inspire others to take action.

expressions.phrase: are able to inspire where others aren't
expressions.meaning: 다른 이들은 못하는 반면 영감을 줄 수 있다
expressions.usage: "where"가 "whereas(반면에)"의 의미로 사용되어 대조를 만든다

patterns.pattern: [Subject] explains why [some] are able to [verb] where others aren't.
patterns.explanation: 성공과 실패를 나누는 원인을 설명하는 패턴. where가 대조 접속사로 기능.
examples: This principle explains why some teachers inspire where others don't.
This framework explains why some brands succeed where others fail.

drillQuestions.question: This idea explains _____ some leaders are able to inspire.
Some leaders inspire _____ others aren't able to.

sentences.notes: "where"가 장소가 아닌 대조(whereas)의 의미로 사용된 점이 핵심. "this little idea"에서 little은 크기가 아닌 "단순함"을 겸손하게 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: why some organizations and some leaders

sentences.original: Every single person, every single organization on the planet knows what they do, 100 percent.
sentences.translation: 지구상의 모든 개인, 모든 조직은 자신이 하는 일이 무엇인지 알고 있습니다. 100퍼센트.

grammarElements.text: Every single person, every single organization on the planet
grammarElements.label: 주어 (강조 반복)

vocabulary.example: Every single student passed the exam.

expressions.usage: "every"만으로 부족할 때 "single"을 추가하여 예외 없음을 강조
확신의 정도를 수치로 표현하여 단정적 주장

patterns.pattern: Every single [A], every single [B] knows [what/how/why] they [verb], [percentage].
patterns.explanation: "every single"을 반복하고 퍼센트를 추가하여 보편성을 극대화하는 패턴.
examples: Every single employee, every single manager knows what they are responsible for, 100 percent.

drillQuestions.question: Every _____ person on the planet knows what they do.
Every single organization knows _____ they do.

sentences.notes: "every single"의 반복은 예외 없음을 강조. "100 percent"를 문장 끝에 독립적으로 배치하여 단정적 확신을 표현. 이후 "some know how" → "very few know why"로 이어지는 점진적 축소 구조의 시작.

koreanAlignment.en: Every single person, every single organization

koreanAlignment.ko: 자신이 하는 일이 무엇인지 알고 있습니다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 점층법(climax)의 시작 — what(100%) → how(some) → why(very few)로 좁혀지는 구조의 첫 단계

sentences.original: But very, very few people or organizations know why they do what they do.
sentences.translation: 하지만 자신이 하는 일의 이유를 아는 사람이나 조직은 극히, 극히 적습니다.

grammarElements.text: very, very few people or organizations
grammarElements.label: 주어 (강조 반복 — very, very)

grammarElements.text: why they do what they do
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (이중 간접의문문 — why + what)

vocabulary.example: Very few people know the truth.

expressions.usage: "very"를 반복하여 희소성을 극적으로 강조

expressions.phrase: why they do what they do
expressions.usage: 행동의 근본적 동기와 목적을 묻는 표현

patterns.pattern: Very, very few [people/organizations] know why they [verb] what they [verb].
patterns.explanation: 대부분이 모르는 근본적 질문을 제시하는 패턴. "why they do what they do"는 강연의 핵심 문구.
examples: Very, very few companies know why they exist beyond making money.
Very, very few teachers know why they teach what they teach.

drillQuestions.question: Very, very _____ people know why they do what they do.
They don't know _____ they do what they do.

sentences.notes: "why they do what they do"는 간접의문문 안에 또 다른 간접의문문이 내포된 구조. 강연의 핵심 메시지를 담은 구절로, 이후 반복적으로 등장한다. "100 percent" → "some" → "very, very few"로 좁혀지는 점층 구조의 클라이맥스.

koreanAlignment.en: But very, very few people or organizations
koreanAlignment.ko: 하지만 극히, 극히 적은 사람이나 조직만이

koreanAlignment.en: know why they do what they do
koreanAlignment.ko: 자신이 하는 일의 이유를 알고 있습니다

pronunciationNotes: very: /ˈveri/ — 두 번 연속 발음할 때 리듬감 있게 강세를 둘 것
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 점층법(climax) 완성 — what(100%) → how(some) → why(very, very few)로 좁혀져 핵심 개념을 드러냄

sentences.original: As a result, the way we think, we act, the way we communicate is from the outside in, it's obvious.
sentences.translation: 결과적으로, 우리가 생각하고, 행동하고, 소통하는 방식은 바깥에서 안쪽으로입니다. 당연한 것이죠.

grammarElements.text: the way we think, we act, the way we communicate
grammarElements.label: 주어 (병렬)

vocabulary.example: The answer was obvious to everyone.

expressions.usage: 앞선 원인에 대한 결과를 소개할 때
골든 서클에서 what → how → why 순서를 시각적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: The way we [verb A], the way we [verb B] is from [direction].
patterns.explanation: 행동 방식을 방향적 비유로 설명하는 패턴. 골든 서클의 핵심 대비.
examples: The way we teach, the way we evaluate is from the top down.
The way we plan, the way we execute is from the outside in.

drillQuestions.question: The way we communicate is from the _____ in.
As a _____, we go from the clearest thing to the fuzziest thing.

sentences.notes: "the way we think, we act"에서 두 번째 "the way"가 생략된 구어적 구조. "from the outside in"은 골든 서클의 what → how → why 방향을 표현하며, "from the inside out"과 대조를 이룬다.

koreanAlignment.en: the way we think, we act, the way we communicate

sentences.original: But the inspired leaders and the inspired organizations—regardless of their size, regardless of their industry—all think, act and communicate from the inside out.
sentences.translation: 하지만 영감을 받은 리더와 조직은—규모에 관계없이, 업종에 관계없이—모두 안쪽에서 바깥으로 생각하고, 행동하고, 소통합니다.

grammarElements.text: the inspired leaders and the inspired organizations
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: regardless of their size, regardless of their industry
grammarElements.label: 삽입구 (양보 — ~에 관계없이)

vocabulary.example: The inspired team delivered exceptional results.
We will proceed regardless of the weather.
The tech industry is growing rapidly.

expressions.usage: 조건이나 차이에 상관없이 동일하게 적용됨을 표현
골든 서클에서 why → how → what 순서를 표현. "from the outside in"의 반대

patterns.pattern: [Subject]—regardless of [A], regardless of [B]—all [verb] from the inside out.
patterns.explanation: 양보 삽입구(regardless of)로 보편성을 강조한 뒤, 핵심 방식을 제시하는 패턴.
examples: Great thinkers—regardless of their field, regardless of their era—all start with fundamental questions.

drillQuestions.question: Regardless _____ their size, they all communicate the same way.
Inspired leaders communicate from the _____ out.

sentences.notes: "from the inside out" vs "from the outside in"은 강연 전체를 관통하는 핵심 대비. 대시(—)로 삽입된 "regardless of"구는 이 원칙이 업종/규모를 초월함을 강조. "think, act and communicate"는 강연에서 반복되는 3동사 공식.

koreanAlignment.en: But the inspired leaders and the inspired organizations

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대조(antithesis) + 반복(anaphora) — "from the outside in" vs "from the inside out", "regardless of"의 반복으로 핵심 메시지를 각인
